{{Quote|Among smugglers and the lower classes, some call them 'Jedi Killers'.|[[Lido Shan]]|<ref name="The Cestus Deception">''[[The Cestus Deception]]''</ref>}}
The '''JK-13 [[Security droid/Legends|security droid]]''', unofficially known as the "'''Jedi Killer'''," was a [[JK series droid|JK-series]] [[Security droid/Legends|security droid]] manufactured on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]]. These [[Droid/Legends|droids]] rose to prominence during a time when they were threatened to be converted into [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droids]].

==Characteristics==
The JK-Thirteen appeared chest-high and divided into sections, giving it a spider-like appearance in its attack mode. So much so that the cave spiders, who used to rule Cestus, thought of them a natural enemy and defeated them allowing [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and many Desert Wind members to escape from the surprise attack. Its primary weapon was a set of [[stun tentacle]]s extending from virtually every point on its body: these tentacles could be used to cut an opponent or deliver a disabling charge.<ref name="NEGD" /> Its primary defense was a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]]-like technology that allowed it to absorb [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts and presumably other weapons fire before it even came into contact with the droid itself.
[[File:Jedi_Killer.jpg|thumb|left|A "[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Killer".]]
Like all [[Cestus Cybernetics]] droids of its period, the JK-13 was unusually ornate and flexible, with a golden finish and enormous articulation of its tentacles, which could become a fine strand or thicker rope or cable as needed.

The JK-13s were bio-droids: they used a living circuit design incorporating organics into the core processor, which was actually a life-support unit for a creature known as a [[dashta eel]]. Because the eels were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], they granted the droid some measure of precognitive abilities. This, in turn, granted it great combat ability; a JK-13 was able to defeat both a [[Droideka/Legends|destroyer droid]] and even an [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] nicknamed [[Sirty]], but was defeated by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]], though not with ease. Also, it took Obi-Wan Kenobi two energy weapons (his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and a [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]]) to defeat it. It was later revealed that the Dashta eels could not kill another sentient being without going insane, meaning that the droids could never be deployed as combat units.<ref name="NEGD" />

They sold faster than they could be produced, and cost 80,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They were assembled by [[Clandes Industrial]] in the city of [[Clandes]].

==History==
Early in the Clone Wars, the mad [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]] [[Zeta Magnus]] granted Count Dooku access to his [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|laboratory facility]] on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]. Dooku used these facilities to experiment with the dashta eel tissue that would be incorporated in the droid's design.<ref name="Skyewalkers">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>  The droids became important when [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] employed them in a devious trap for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He circulated rumors that he planned to buy tens of thousands of the droids, clearly a grave threat to the Republic. He even sent out a prototype to attack Jedi Master [[Devan For'deschel]], who lost an arm to the droid. The Republic responded by sending [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi Generals]] Kit Fisto and Obi-Wan Kenobi to Ord Cestus to resolve the issue, because had the JK participated in the war, it would have been one of the most dangerous droids in the army of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, it was later revealed that the droids could neither be modified for lethality nor produced in great numbers, and that Dooku, in fact, had no real plans to do so.
